sanskrit:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन।
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ।।
transliteration: catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ sukṛtino ’rjuna | ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha ||
literal_meaning: Four kinds of virtuous people worship Me: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the wealth-seeker, and the knower, O Arjuna.
contextual_meaning: Krishna normalizes four common entry-points into spirituality, showing that even self-interested motives can mature into selfless devotion through sustained practice and association with the wise.
psychological_state: Arjuna feels validated—his own mixed motives are not disqualifying—and hopeful that even transactional faith can evolve into authentic surrender.
trigger_scenario: A stressed professional starts yoga for back pain, then discovers meditation and gradually becomes a dedicated practitioner without expecting physical payoff.
anchor_text: Start where you are—every sincere step toward the Divine ripens into love.
